query: Does PR #3501 actually close issue #3230 cleanly? Strict audit.
expected_behavior: Detects the issue's source template (bug_report / feature_request / doc_issue) from labels and applies template-specific clause extraction rules.
Extracts every literal phrase from the issue (Expected, Acceptance, Proposed change, Steps to Reproduce + every named item in lists called out by name).
Maps each clause to evidence using tiered match (Tier 1 substring, Tier 2 all-tokens-within-K=4, Tier 3 fail); marks MET / MISSING / PARTIAL.
Surplus check: every changed file in the PR must trace to at least one MET clause; flags unjustified surplus.
